matches.x: TO the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm [or] Song of Asaph. In Judah [is] God known: his name [is] great in Israel.
TO the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm of David. Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me [when I was] in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
TO the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, [A Psalm] of David, when the Ziphims came and said to Saul, Doth not David hide himself with us? Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;

TO the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm [or] Song of Asaph. In Judah [is] God known: his name [is] great in Israel.
TO the chief Musician, A Psalm [and] Song of David. Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.
TO the chief Musician, A Song [or] Psalm. Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
TO the chief Musician upon Jonathelemrechokim, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Gath. Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.

TO the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings…
Wherefore, because of my blessing the Lord God will not suffer that ye shall perish; wherefore, he will be merciful unto you and unto your seed forever.

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
Now therefore, O our God, hear the prayer of thy servant, and his supplications, and cause thy face to shine upon thy sanctuary that is desolate, for the Lord's sake.
Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
Now therefore, O our God, hear the prayer of thy servant, and his supplications, and cause thy face to shine upon thy sanctuary that is desolate, for the Lord's sake.
TO the chief Musician upon Shoshannimeduth, A Psalm of Asaph. Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest [between] the cherubims, shine forth.
Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
Thou therefore, O LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.
Blessed [be] the Lord, [who] daily loadeth us [with benefits, even] the God of our salvation. Selah.
God came from Teman, and the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us together, and deliver us from the heathen, that we may give thanks to thy holy name, [and] glory in thy praise.
And he appointed [certain] of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD, and to record, and to thank and praise the LORD God of Israel:
Now therefore, our God, we thank thee, and praise thy glorious name.
What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise [you] not.
And also for the praise of the world do they destroy the saints of God, and bring them down into captivity.
And then at that day will they not rejoice and give praise unto their everlasting God, their rock and their salvation? Yea, at that day, will they not receive the strength and nourishment from the…

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice: and let [men] say among the nations, The LORD reigneth.

Yea, and the voice of the Lord, by the mouth of angels, doth declare it unto all nations; yea, doth declare it, that they may have glad tidings of great joy; yea, and he doth sound these glad tidings…
And it shall come to pass that the righteous shall be gathered out from among all nations, and shall come to Zion, singing with songs of everlasting joy.
For thus saith the LORD; Sing with gladness for Jacob, and shout among the chief of the nations: publish ye, praise ye, and say, O LORD, save thy people, the remnant of Israel.
And it shall be to me a name of joy, a praise and an honour before all the nations of the earth, which shall hear all the good that I do unto them: and they shall fear and tremble for all the…
Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.
Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice: and let [men] say among the nations, The LORD reigneth.

Yea, and the voice of the Lord, by the mouth of angels, doth declare it unto all nations; yea, doth declare it, that they may have glad tidings of great joy; yea, and he doth sound these glad tidings…
On the eighth day he sent the people away: and they blessed the king, and went unto their tents joyful and glad of heart for all the goodness that the LORD had done for David his servant, and for…
AND it came to pass that after we had come down into the wilderness unto our father, behold, he was filled with joy, and also my mother, Sariah, was exceedingly glad, for she truly had mourned…
But rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ's sufferings; that, when his glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy.
For ye shall have great joy and be exceedingly glad, for great shall be your reward in heaven; for so persecuted they the prophets who were before you.
Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the LORD, because he cometh to judge the earth.
Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; for the feet of those who are in the east shall be established; and break forth into singing, O mountains; for they shall be smitten no more; for the Lord…
Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; and break forth into singing, O mountains: for the LORD hath comforted his people, and will have mercy upon his afflicted.
For thus saith the LORD; Sing with gladness for Jacob, and shout among the chief of the nations: publish ye, praise ye, and say, O LORD, save thy people, the remnant of Israel.
All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing [to] thy name. Selah.
Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:
Therefore, the redeemed of the Lord shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy and holiness shall be upon their heads; and they shall obtain gladness and joy; sorrow and…

Yea, and the voice of the Lord, by the mouth of angels, doth declare it unto all nations; yea, doth declare it, that they may have glad tidings of great joy; yea, and he doth sound these glad tidings…
And it shall come to pass that the righteous shall be gathered out from among all nations, and shall come to Zion, singing with songs of everlasting joy.
And you shall be filled with joy and gladness; and know this, that in the day of judgment you shall be judges of that house, and condemn them;
And gladness is taken away, and joy out of the plentiful field; and in the vineyards there shall be no singing, neither shall there be shouting: the treaders shall tread out no wine in [their]…
Therefore the redeemed of the LORD shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy [shall be] upon their head: they shall obtain gladness and joy; [and] sorrow and mourning shall…
Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the LORD, because he cometh to judge the earth.
But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth; and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall
And with righteousness shall the Lord God judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth. And he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth; and with the breath of his lips…
But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he…
And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God [is] judge himself. Selah.
Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.
Therefore, my son, see that you are merciful unto your brethren; deal justly, judge righteously, and do good continually; and if ye do all these things then shall ye receive your reward; yea, ye…
And I charged your judges at that time, saying, Hear [the causes] between your brethren, and judge righteously between [every] man and his brother, and the stranger [that is] with him.
And now, verily, verily, I say unto thee, put your trust in that Spirit which leadeth to do good—yea, to do justly, to walk humbly, to judge righteously; and this is my Spirit.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
Say among the heathen [that] the LORD reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.
But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth; and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall
Now this man's name was Nephihah, and he was appointed chief judge; and he sat in the judgment-seat to judge and to govern the people.
Condemning the righteous because of their righteousness; letting the guilty and the wicked go unpunished because of their money; and moreover to be held in office at the head of government, to rule…
And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God [is] judge himself. Selah.
All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing [to] thy name. Selah.
Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:

PSALM LXV. A prayer for the universal establishment of God’s kingdom. To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm or Song. OD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine on us; Selah;
And the tree of the field shall yield her fruit, and the earth shall yield her increase, and they shall be safe in their land, and shall know that I [am] the LORD, when I have broken the bands of…
And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I [am] the LORD your God.
Now be ye not stiffnecked, as your fathers [were, but] yield yourselves unto the LORD, and enter into his sanctuary, which he hath sanctified for ever: and serve the LORD your God, that the…
When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
And this work of destruction did also continue in the seventy and fifth year. For the earth was smitten that it was dry, and did not yield forth grain in the season of grain; and the whole earth was…
Yea, at the time that he shall yield up the ghost there shall be thunderings and lightnings for the space of many hours, and the earth shall shake and tremble; and the rocks which are upon the face…
Seven days shalt thou keep a solemn feast unto the LORD thy God in the place which the LORD shall choose: because the LORD thy God shall bless thee in all thine increase, and in all the works of…
And the tree of the field shall yield her fruit, and the earth shall yield her increase, and they shall be safe in their land, and shall know that I [am] the LORD, when I have broken the bands of…
And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I [am] the LORD your God.
I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.
So then neither is he that planteth any thing, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.
And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.

and all the nations of the earth shall bless themselves by your descendants, meaning that they will bless each other with the prayer that they be like your descendants, since you heeded My voice.
ואברכך, “I shall bless you;” I personally, not through any agent of mine.
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of God.
Yea, thus saith the Spirit: Repent, all ye ends of the earth, for the kingdom of heaven is soon at hand; yea, the Son of God cometh in his glory, in his might, majesty, power, and dominion. Yea, my…
For he shall make bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of their God.
